steps.title: Identify the structures at the sentence boundary
steps.content: Left: independent clause ending with “study its ancient tombs.” Right: participial phrase “built into the rocky outcrops of a vast desert” followed by independent clause “these burial chambers seem to blend seamlessly with nature.”

steps.title: Apply the comma splice rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses cannot be joined by a comma alone—this violates the comma splice rule.

Choice A — tombs. Built separates them with a period, allowing the participial phrase to introduce the second sentence.

The other choices create comma splices by joining independent clauses with only a comma or produce an incoherent parallel structure.

That matches Choice A — tombs. Built.
